conjecture_title: Open Problem (multitasker at average degree $\Theta(\log n)$)
summary: No paper resolving the open problem has been found. The follow-up work arXiv:1809.02835 (Multitasking Capacity: Hardness Results and Improved Constructions, published in SIAM J. Discrete Math.) proves hardness of approximation results and provides improved constructions achieving $\alpha = 1/2 - \varepsilon$ for matchings of size $n/\mathrm{poly}(d)$, but does not construct multitaskers with $\alpha > 0$ independent of $n$ at average degree $\Theta(\log n)$. The specific threshold question — whether $\Theta(\log n)$ average degree suffices for constant $\alpha$ — remains open as of the search date.

since_posted.title: Multitasking Capacity: Hardness Results and Improved Constructions
since_posted.authors: Noga Alon, Jonathan D. Cohen, Biswadip Dey, Tom Griffiths, Sebastian Musslick, Kayhan Ozcimder, Daniel Reichman, Igor Shinkar, Tal Wagner
since_posted.venue: arXiv preprint (published in SIAM Journal on Discrete Mathematics)
since_posted.claim: Proves computational hardness for approximating multitasking capacity and gives improved constructions achieving $\alpha = 1/2 - \varepsilon$ for matchings of size $n/\mathrm{poly}(d)$, but does not resolve the $\Theta(\log n)$ average-degree threshold question.

search_queries: multitasker graph theory "average degree" "log n" multitasking capacity Alon Reichman arXiv 1611.02400
graph-theoretic multitasking "multitasking capacity" "independent set" alpha Musslick Shinkar follow-up
"multitasking capacity" "log n" graph multitasker construction Reichman Shinkar Wagner 2018 2019 2020
notes: The main follow-up paper arXiv:1809.02835 was verified via WebFetch; it improves constructions and proves hardness but does not address the specific open problem about $\Theta(\log n)$ average degree. No other paper specifically resolving this threshold question was found in the search.
